title: inula japonica
intro: Inula japonica, also known as Japanese elecampane, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that may help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. Inula japonica is believed to offer various health benefits, such as alleviating respiratory problems, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in herbal remedies and supplements, although its efficacy and safety need further scientific validation.
